soc.description: Chip/silicon part number, e.g. cq2390, qcs6490 — NOT necessarily the same as soc_family

soc_family.description: meta-qcom's internal SoC codename used to name the shared conf/machine/include/qcom-<soc_family>.inc file and SOC_FAMILY variable (e.g. shikra, glymur, hamoa, kaanapali, purwa, qcm2290). Often differs from `soc` (the literal chip part number) and cannot be inferred from either the machine or board name. Null only when this is a genuinely new SoC family with no existing qcom-<soc_family>.inc yet — the consumer must scaffold one using a sensible codename.

reference_board.description: Existing machine this spec was modeled on

fit_dtb_compatible.description: Maps compatible strings to kernel_devicetree entries for conf/machine/include/fit-dtb-compatible.inc's FIT_DTB_COMPATIBLE[...] flags. do_generate_qcom_fitimage uses these to emit the FIT image's configurations node; without a matching entry for a DTB combo, the FIT image still builds (only a bb.note is logged) but with zero conf-N nodes, and UEFI's ParseFitDt then has nothing to match at boot and falls back to a broken default config. Null only when every kernel_devicetree entry is already covered by an existing FIT_DTB_COMPATIBLE entry for this soc_family (e.g. a firmware/config variant reusing another board's DTBs verbatim) — do not leave null just because the entries haven't been checked.

compatible.description: Compatible string UEFI's ParseFitDt matches at boot, e.g. qcom,shikracqm-itp. Becomes the FIT_DTB_COMPATIBLE[...] flag name with ',' encoded as '_'.

dtbs.description: Base DTB stem followed by any overlay stem(s), space-separated in the .inc file's flag value — matching kernel_devicetree entries minus the .dtb/.dtbo suffix, e.g. ["shikra-cqm-evk", "shikra-cqm-evk-imx577-camera"]

partition_files_subdir.description: Must equal partitions/<machine>/<partition_conf.storage_type> so qcom-partition-conf's do_deploy discovers the artifacts

soc_kernel_modules.description: packagegroup-machine-essential.bb's RRECOMMENDS:${PN}-qcom-<soc_family>-soc entries — the SoC-family-level kernel modules pulled in for every board on this SoC. Populate only when scaffolding a genuinely new soc_family (no existing packagegroup-machine-essential.bb entry yet); null when the board reuses an existing soc_family whose ${PN}-qcom-<soc_family>-soc entry is already present (the common case for a new board on an existing SoC).

packagegroup.description: Firmware/DSP package lists for recipes-bsp/packagegroups/packagegroup-<machine>.bb. Null only when the board reuses an existing packagegroup (e.g. a firmware/config variant of another machine) instead of needing its own.

compatible_machine.description: COMPATIBLE_MACHINE regex override on the packagegroup recipe, e.g. "(qcom)". Null when the recipe doesn't set this variable at all (the common case — most packagegroup-<machine>.bb recipes omit it).

firmware_packages.description: Entries for the -firmware package (dependency type given by firmware_packages_dependency_type), e.g. linux-firmware-qcom-<soc_family>-compute

firmware_packages_dependency_type.description: Whether firmware_packages are wired up via RDEPENDS or RRECOMMENDS on ${PN}-firmware. Most boards use RRECOMMENDS; check the reference board's recipe rather than assuming.

hexagon_dsp_packages.description: Entries for the -hexagon-dsp-binaries package (dependency type given by hexagon_dsp_packages_dependency_type), one per DSP-bearing DTB variant

hexagon_dsp_packages_dependency_type.description: Whether hexagon_dsp_packages are wired up via RDEPENDS or RRECOMMENDS on ${PN}-hexagon-dsp-binaries. Most boards (qcm6490-idp, sa8775p-ride) use RDEPENDS; some outliers (kaanapali-mtp) use RRECOMMENDS for both package sets — check the reference board's recipe rather than assuming.

optional_feature_packages.description: Packages gated by bb.utils.contains(_any)('DISTRO_FEATURES', ...) — null a key when that feature has no gated package on this board

rt_isolation.description: RT CPU isolation block. Null/needed=false for boards without an RT kernel config.

firmware_boot.description: Download facts for recipes-bsp/firmware-boot/firmware-qcom-boot-<boot_firmware-suffix>[_<pv>].bb + matching .inc, needed to scaffold the recipe when it doesn't yet exist in the consumer's tree. Null only when boot_firmware is null (no boot-firmware recipe at all, e.g. vendor-flashed-independently boards) or the recipe is a firmware/config variant reusing another board's recipe verbatim.

fw_artifactory.description: FW_ARTIFACTORY value — artifactory path (without scheme) the bootbinaries zip is fetched from

bootbinaries.description: BOOTBINARIES value — zip basename prefix, also the unpacked subdir name under UNPACKDIR

pv.description: Recipe PV — becomes the _<pv>.bb filename suffix and the zip's version segment in SRC_URI

license.description: LICENSE value, e.g. LICENSE.qcom-2. Null if the board's boot-firmware recipe uses a different LIC_FILES_CHKSUM shape than the qcom-2-inside-zip pattern — say so in notes.

license_md5sum.description: LIC_FILES_CHKSUM md5 for file://${UNPACKDIR}/${BOOTBINARIES}/LICENSE.qcom-2.txt. Null if licensing is sourced differently (e.g. a separately-downloaded LICENSE.txt with its own sha256sum) — explain in notes.

qcom_boot_img_subdir.description: QCOM_BOOT_IMG_SUBDIR value, normally equal to machine_creation.boot_files_subdir

firmware_cdt.description: Download facts for recipes-bsp/firmware-boot/firmware-qcom-cdt-<cdt_firmware-suffix>.bb, needed to scaffold the recipe when it doesn't yet exist in the consumer's tree. Null only when cdt_firmware is null.

cdt_artifactory.description: CDT_ARTIFACTORY override, if this board doesn't use firmware-qcom-cdt-common.inc's shared default (artifacts.codelinaro.org/artifactory/codelinaro-le/Qualcomm_Linux)

download_path.description: Path segment appended after CDT_ARTIFACTORY in SRC_URI, e.g. CQ2390/cdt/cq2390-itp.zip

downloadfilename_prefix.description: Prefix before _${PV}.zip in SRC_URI's downloadfilename= param, e.g. cq2390-itp

qcom_cdt_subdir.description: QCOM_CDT_SUBDIR value, normally equal to machine_creation.boot_files_subdir
